intro: Amphistelma aphyllum, also known as Devil's Claw, is a succulent plant native to South Africa, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which have been used to alleviate various health issues such as arthritis, fever, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Amphistelma aphyllum include harpagoside, a key compound responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other glycosides and iridoids. In traditional herbal medicine, Amphistelma aphyllum is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or powder, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
